newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Autonomous research agents can now draft hypotheses, write code, run experiments, and produce papers, but they remain brittle when experiments fail. Under the prevailing paradigm, failure recovery is usually delegated to a single free-form reflection: a rich trajectory of metrics, logs, and design choices is compres...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: One Reflection Is Not Enough: Self-Correcting Autonomous Research via Multi-Hypothesis Failure Attribution. Jie Ma, Binfei Chu, Jie Gao, Jinlu Zhang, Yiwei Ma, Yi Tan, Jiayi Ji, Xiaoshuai Sun, Rongrong Ji. 2026-06-30. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31478v1
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Studies source-grounded research workflows, citation verification, or evidence-backed research reports.
newly_discovered_papers.title: One Reflection Is Not Enough: Self-Correcting Autonomous Research via Multi-Hypothesis Failure Attribution

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Ideation plays a pivotal role in scientific discovery. Recent LLM, especially AI Scientist systems, show promising potential for automated ideation. However, existing approaches predominantly rely on pre-defined agentic workflows. This constraint severely limits the flexibility required to navigate the vast search s...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: Agentic-Ideation: Sample Efficient Agentic Trajectories Synthesis for Scientific Ideation Agents. Keyu Zhao, Lingyan Kong, Fengli Xu, Yong Li. 2026-06-30. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31229v1
newly_discovered_papers.title: Agentic-Ideation: Sample Efficient Agentic Trajectories Synthesis for Scientific Ideation Agents

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: AI-assisted research has entered a stage in which the central question is not only whether systems can generate hypotheses, run experiments, or produce manuscripts, but whether their scientific claims are calibrated to the evidence that supports them. This Perspective-style paper develops a conceptual and methodolog...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: The Calibration Turn in AI-Assisted Research: A Conceptual and Methodological Framework for Evidence-Licensed Claims. Hongmin Li. 2026-06-30. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31273v1
newly_discovered_papers.title: The Calibration Turn in AI-Assisted Research: A Conceptual and Methodological Framework for Evidence-Licensed Claims

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Discovering governing equations directly from observational data is a key step towards interpretable scientific machine learning. Current data-driven approaches typically operate on a single dataset, inherently limiting their performance when faced with restricted observations. In practice, multiple datasets are oft...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: Joint discovery of governing partial differential equations from multi-source datasets by competitive optimization. Hao Xu, Siyu Lou, Yuntian Chen, Dongxiao Zhang. 2026-06-29. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.30699v1
newly_discovered_papers.title: Joint discovery of governing partial differential equations from multi-source datasets by competitive optimization

newly_discovered_papers.abstract_summary: Recent work shows that LLM agents can improve sharp-constant inequalities by searching for extremal constructions, which yield upper bounds. We address the complementary side: a lower bound holds for every admissible function and follows from a convex relaxation of the nonconvex problem, with tighter relaxations giv...
newly_discovered_papers.citation: AI-Assisted Discovery of Convex Relaxations via Dual Agents. Sungyoon Kim, Mert Pilanci. 2026-06-30. https://arxiv.org/abs/2606.31182v1
newly_discovered_papers.relevance_reason: Review candidate: may support deep research workflows but needs human judgment.
newly_discovered_papers.title: AI-Assisted Discovery of Convex Relaxations via Dual Agents
